political.s: <cite index="63-2,63-3">CSX contributed $1,025,683 in the 2024 cycle with $1,530,760 in lobbying spending</cite>. The company's political action committee historically leans toward whichever party holds power, though PAC contributions have traditionally favored Republicans. The verified FEC data shows bipartisan lean with $883K raised.

charity.s: <cite index="37-1">CSX provides $10-12 million in annual charitable contributions through corporate, foundation and employee donations with thousands of volunteer hours</cite>. <cite index="37-10,37-11">The company gave a $5 million gift to the Baltimore and Ohio Railroad Museum toward its $30 million capital campaign</cite>. Focus areas include military/veterans support and community development.

environment.s: <cite index="13-2,13-22">CSX actively participates in the EPA Smartway program and is included in the Dow Jones Sustainability Index and CDP's Carbon Performance Leadership Index</cite>. <cite index="18-1,18-2">The company became the first railroad to achieve a Science Based Target Initiative-approved 2-degree pathway, committing to reduce GHG emissions intensity by 37.3% between 2014 and 2030</cite>. Recent hydrogen fuel cell locomotive pilot demonstrates forward momentum.

labor.s: <cite index="22-1">Federal officials have found CSX Transportation violating federal whistleblower regulations and retaliating against workers who reported safety concerns in multiple instances</cite>. <cite index="22-7,22-8">A 2024 federal judge ruled CSX must pay $453,510 to two workers wrongfully terminated for reporting safety concerns and reinstate them</cite>. Pattern of retaliation rulings over 15+ years reveals systemic labor relations challenges.

dei.s: <cite index="42-1">CSX's focus on diversity, equity and inclusion has received national recognition</cite>. <cite index="49-6,49-7,49-8">The company achieved top Disability Equality Index recognition for sixth consecutive year, with vice president of human resources citing ONE CSX culture and dedication to supporting employees with disabilities</cite>. Multiple employee business resource groups support different communities.

animals.s: Not applicable to freight rail company operations.

guns.s: Not applicable to freight rail transportation company.

privacy.s: Limited public disclosure regarding data privacy practices beyond general corporate privacy policies. CSX website references standard privacy policy but minimal detail on data handling, encryption standards, or third-party data sharing practices available publicly.

execPay.s: <cite index="56-1,56-5,56-6">CEO Joe Hinrichs received $23.7 million total compensation in 2025 including $8.25 million severance when replaced in September</cite>. <cite index="60-1,60-2">Average employee salary is $135K annually while CEO/executive compensation packages routinely exceed $10-23M</cite>. Pay ratio significantly exceeds recommended alignment with worker compensation.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
